questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Speed up to clear the intersection
The light is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that the signal is changing to red, so you should stop when it is safe to do so.

questions.prompt: What shape is a stop sign?
questions.explanation: An eight-sided red octagon always means come to a complete stop.

questions.prompt: A flashing red traffic light means:
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop, then proceed when safe, as at a stop sign
The light is broken; ignore it
Yield only to vehicles on your left
questions.explanation: A flashing red light is treated exactly like a stop sign: you must stop and then go when it is safe.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light tells you to slow down and proceed carefully through the intersection.

questions.prompt: A yellow diamond-shaped sign generally indicates:
choices: A regulatory law you must obey
A warning of a hazard or condition ahead
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond signs warn drivers about hazards or changing conditions ahead.

questions.prompt: What color and shape identifies a yield sign?
questions.explanation: A yield sign is a downward-pointing triangle that tells you to slow down and give the right-of-way.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the center of the road means:
choices: Passing is allowed if traffic is clear
The lane is for turning only
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited there.

questions.prompt: What does a green arrow signal at an intersection mean?
choices: You may proceed in the direction of the arrow with the right-of-way
questions.explanation: A green arrow gives you a protected movement in the direction the arrow points.

questions.prompt: White lane lines on a roadway separate:
choices: Traffic moving in the same direction
The road from the shoulder only
questions.explanation: White lines divide lanes of traffic traveling in the same direction.

questions.prompt: An orange sign or cone on the road usually indicates:
questions.explanation: Orange is used for construction and work-zone signs and warnings.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, if two vehicles arrive at the same time, who goes first?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop together, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When you are turning left, you must yield to:
questions.explanation: A driver turning left must give the right-of-way to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians crossing the road being entered.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren on, you should:
choices: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull to the right and stop until it passes
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right edge of the road and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: In New Mexico, a right turn on a red light is:
choices: Allowed after a complete stop unless a sign prohibits it
questions.explanation: You may turn right on red after a full stop and yielding, unless a posted sign prohibits the turn.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at a marked or unmarked crosswalk?
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield to pedestrians in both marked and unmarked crosswalks.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout or traffic circle, you should:
choices: Yield to traffic already in the circle
Stop in the circle to let others in
Drive the wrong way if it is shorter
questions.explanation: Vehicles already in a roundabout have the right-of-way over those entering it.

questions.prompt: At an intersection with no signs or signals, you should:
choices: Slow down and be ready to yield, especially to traffic on your right
Stop and wait for a police officer
questions.explanation: At uncontrolled intersections you must slow down and yield, generally to the vehicle on your right.

questions.prompt: Before making any turn, you should signal:
choices: Only if other cars are nearby
Well in advance of the turn
questions.explanation: Signaling in advance warns other drivers and pedestrians of your intentions.

questions.prompt: When you want to turn left from a two-way street, you should generally turn from:
choices: The lane closest to the center line
questions.explanation: Left turns should begin from the lane nearest the center line of the road.

questions.prompt: A driver entering a road from a driveway or alley must:
choices: Yield to traffic and pedestrians already on the road
Have the right-of-way over the road
Stop all traffic on the road
questions.explanation: When entering a roadway from a driveway or alley, you must yield to vehicles and pedestrians already there.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, the speed limit in a New Mexico business or residential district is:
questions.explanation: New Mexico's default speed limit in business and residential districts is 30 mph unless signs say otherwise.

questions.prompt: What is a common maximum speed limit on New Mexico interstate highways?
questions.explanation: Interstate highways in New Mexico are often posted at up to 75 mph.

questions.prompt: The three-second rule helps a driver maintain a safe:
questions.explanation: The three-second rule is a simple way to keep a safe following distance behind the vehicle ahead.

questions.prompt: The Basic Speed Law requires drivers to:
choices: Drive at a speed that is reasonable and prudent for conditions
questions.explanation: Even at or below the posted limit, you must adjust your speed for weather, traffic, and road conditions.

questions.prompt: In rain, snow, or fog, you should adjust your following distance by:
questions.explanation: Poor conditions reduce traction and visibility, so you need extra following distance to stop safely.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than the flow of traffic can be dangerous because it:
choices: Disrupts traffic flow and can cause crashes
questions.explanation: Going too slowly interferes with the normal movement of traffic and can lead to collisions.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, a typical speed limit on a rural New Mexico highway is about:
questions.explanation: Rural highways in New Mexico are commonly posted around 55 mph unless signs indicate another limit.

questions.prompt: Posted speed limits represent the maximum speed:
choices: You must drive at all times
questions.explanation: Speed limits assume good weather, light traffic, and dry roads; you must slow down when conditions are worse.

questions.prompt: As your speed increases, your total stopping distance:
questions.explanation: Higher speeds require much longer distances to perceive, react, and brake to a stop.

questions.prompt: You should slow down before:
questions.explanation: Reducing speed before a curve helps you keep control, since braking hard in the curve can cause a skid.

questions.prompt: You must never pass another vehicle when:
choices: The road is straight and clear
You are on a hill or curve where you cannot see far enough ahead
There is a broken yellow line on your side
questions.explanation: Passing where your view is blocked by a hill or curve is unsafe and illegal.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, slower traffic should generally:
choices: Keep to the right except when passing
Drive in the center lane only
questions.explanation: Keeping right except to pass lets faster traffic flow safely in the left lane.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal, check mirrors, and check your blind spot
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking mirrors and blind spots prevents collisions with vehicles you cannot otherwise see.

questions.prompt: A center left-turn lane (shared, with yellow markings) may be used for:
questions.explanation: A two-way center left-turn lane is reserved for turning left, not for passing or traveling.

questions.prompt: After you pass a vehicle, you should return to your lane when:
choices: You can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
questions.explanation: Returning only when the passed vehicle is visible in your mirror ensures you have enough room.

questions.prompt: When a school bus has its red lights flashing and stop arm out, you must:
choices: Stop and wait until the lights stop flashing
questions.explanation: You must stop for a school bus loading or unloading children and not pass until its signals turn off.

questions.prompt: It is illegal to pass at or near:
questions.explanation: Passing at intersections and railroad crossings is prohibited because of the high risk of conflict.

questions.prompt: To complete a pass, you should:
choices: Exceed the speed limit if needed
questions.explanation: You must complete a pass without breaking the speed limit; if you cannot, do not attempt the pass.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow center line means:
choices: Passing is allowed when it is safe
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line on your side allows passing when the way ahead is clear and safe.

questions.prompt: New Mexico's Move Over law requires drivers approaching a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights to:
choices: Change lanes away from it, or slow down if changing lanes is not possible
questions.explanation: The Move Over law protects roadside workers by requiring you to move over a lane or slow to a safe speed.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a bicyclist, you should:
choices: Give them a safe passing distance
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have a right to the road, so pass them only with a safe amount of space.

questions.prompt: Large trucks have large blind spots, so you should:
choices: Avoid driving directly behind or alongside them
Cut in front right after passing
questions.explanation: Staying out of a truck's blind spots ensures the driver can see you and react safely.

questions.prompt: Motorcycles can be harder to see, so drivers should:
choices: Treat them as if they are not there
Allow them a full lane and check carefully for them
Pass them as closely as possible
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are entitled to a full lane, and extra attention helps you spot these smaller vehicles.

questions.prompt: After passing a large truck, you should not return to its lane until you:
choices: Are right in front of its bumper
Can see the whole truck in your rearview mirror
questions.explanation: Cutting in too soon leaves the truck no room to stop, so wait until you can see it fully in your mirror.

questions.prompt: Near schools, crosswalks, and bus stops, drivers should:
choices: Drive faster to clear the area
Slow down and watch carefully for pedestrians
Use the horn instead of braking
questions.explanation: These areas have many pedestrians, especially children, so reduced speed and attention are essential.

questions.prompt: Large trucks generally need more room to:
questions.explanation: Because of their size and weight, trucks require extra distance to stop and often swing wide to turn.

questions.prompt: When a pedestrian who is blind is crossing with a white cane or guide dog, you must:
choices: Yield and let them cross safely
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield the right-of-way to pedestrians using a white cane or guide dog.

questions.prompt: When you see a vehicle stopped on the shoulder with hazard lights, you should:
choices: Move over a lane if you can, or slow down
questions.explanation: Giving room to stopped vehicles protects people who may be standing near the roadway.

questions.prompt: In New Mexico, the legal BAC limit for a driver age 21 or older is:
questions.explanation: Drivers 21 and older are legally impaired at a blood alcohol concentration of 0.08 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: Under New Mexico's zero-tolerance law, a driver under 21 can lose their license with a BAC as low as:
questions.explanation: For drivers under 21, a BAC of 0.02 percent or higher can lead to license revocation.

questions.prompt: The only thing that will reliably lower your blood alcohol concentration is:
questions.explanation: Only time allows your body to remove alcohol; coffee, food, and air do not speed up the process.

questions.prompt: New Mexico law prohibits drivers from:
choices: Reading or manually typing a text message on a handheld device while driving
questions.explanation: It is illegal to read, view, or type text messages on a handheld device while driving in New Mexico.

questions.prompt: By driving on New Mexico roads, you give 'implied consent' to:
choices: Chemical testing for alcohol or drugs when lawfully requested
questions.explanation: Implied consent means refusing a lawful chemical test carries its own penalties, such as license revocation.

questions.prompt: Alcohol affects a driver by:
choices: Impairing judgment, coordination, and reaction time
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reactions and weakens judgment and coordination even before reaching the legal limit.

questions.prompt: Prescription and over-the-counter medicines can:
choices: Impair your ability to drive safely
Only affect driving if mixed with food
questions.explanation: Many medications cause drowsiness or slowed reactions, so check labels before driving.

questions.prompt: To avoid distraction, you should set your GPS route:
questions.explanation: Setting your route before you start keeps your attention on driving rather than the device.

questions.prompt: The safest choice if you plan to drink is to:
choices: Arrange a sober driver or other transportation
questions.explanation: Planning ahead for a sober ride is the only way to be sure you do not drive impaired.

questions.prompt: A good rule for using your headlights is to turn them on whenever you:
questions.explanation: Turning headlights on with your wipers keeps you visible to others in rain, snow, or fog.

questions.prompt: In heavy fog, you should use:
questions.explanation: High beams reflect off fog and reduce visibility, so low beams work better in fog.

questions.prompt: If a sudden dust storm or blinding rain makes driving unsafe in New Mexico, you should:
choices: Speed up to get through it
Pull completely off the road, stop, and turn off your lights
questions.explanation: Pulling fully off the road and turning off lights keeps others from following you off the roadway in a dust storm.

questions.prompt: If your car starts to skid, you should:
choices: Steer in the direction you want the front of the car to go
Turn the wheel sharply the opposite way
questions.explanation: Easing off the gas and steering where you want to go helps you regain control without overcorrecting.

questions.prompt: Bridges and overpasses are dangerous in cold weather because they:
questions.explanation: Air flows above and below bridges, so they ice over before regular roads in cold conditions.

questions.prompt: On wet or icy roads, you should:
questions.explanation: Cruise control can cause loss of traction on slippery roads, so control your speed manually.

questions.prompt: When visibility is reduced by rain or snow, you should:
choices: Slow down and increase your following distance
questions.explanation: Reducing speed and adding space gives you more time to react when you cannot see well.

questions.prompt: You should use your headlights:
choices: From dusk to dawn and when visibility is poor
questions.explanation: Headlights at night and in poor visibility help you see and be seen by others.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb when facing downhill stops the car from rolling into traffic if the brakes fail.

questions.prompt: It is illegal to park:
choices: In front of a fire hydrant or in a crosswalk
questions.explanation: Parking is prohibited where it blocks hydrants, crosswalks, and other safety areas.

questions.prompt: Before opening your car door into traffic, you should:
choices: Check for approaching cars and bicyclists
questions.explanation: Checking for traffic prevents your door from striking a passing vehicle or cyclist.

questions.prompt: Before leaving your parked vehicle, you should:
choices: Set the parking brake and shift to park
questions.explanation: Setting the brake and shifting to park keeps the vehicle from rolling away.

questions.prompt: Disabled-parking spaces may be used only by vehicles:
choices: Of any driver in a hurry
Displaying a valid disabled placard or plate
questions.explanation: Only vehicles with a valid placard or plate may use accessible parking spaces.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Pointing the wheels away from the curb lets the tire catch the curb if the car rolls back.

questions.prompt: Before pulling away from a curb into traffic, you should:
choices: Signal and yield to traffic already on the road
Wait for a green light only
questions.explanation: Signaling and yielding to passing traffic prevents a collision as you re-enter the roadway.

questions.prompt: On a road without special lane markings, you should normally drive:
choices: On the right side, keeping right except to pass
questions.explanation: Driving on the right and keeping right except to pass is the basic rule of the road.

questions.prompt: In New Mexico, the minimum age to apply for an instructional (learner's) permit is:
questions.explanation: New Mexico teens may apply for an instructional permit at age 15.

questions.prompt: While driving on an instructional permit, you must be accompanied by:
choices: A licensed driver at least 21 years old who has been licensed at least three years
questions.explanation: A qualified adult supervisor 21 or older with three years of licensure must ride beside a permit holder.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving practice must a New Mexico permit holder complete?
questions.explanation: New Mexico requires at least 50 hours of supervised practice, with 10 of those hours at night.

questions.prompt: A New Mexico provisional license holder may not drive between which hours unless supervised?
questions.explanation: Provisional drivers may not drive between midnight and 5 a.m. unless a licensed adult 21 or older is with them.

questions.prompt: Under the provisional license passenger restriction, a teen driver may carry no more than:
choices: One passenger under 21 who is not an immediate family member
questions.explanation: A provisional driver may have at most one passenger under 21 who is not immediate family, unless a qualified adult is present.

questions.prompt: The New Mexico written knowledge test is taken:
choices: In person at an MVD office
questions.explanation: The knowledge test for a permit is administered in person at a New Mexico MVD office.

questions.prompt: If a law enforcement officer asks, a licensed driver must:
choices: Show only a phone photo of an unrelated ID
questions.explanation: Drivers must carry and present their license when asked by law enforcement.

questions.prompt: New Mexico's graduated driver licensing (GDL) system is designed to:
choices: Make licenses harder to get for adults
Gradually introduce new teen drivers to driving with phased restrictions
questions.explanation: GDL eases teens into driving in stages, relaxing restrictions as they gain experience.

questions.prompt: New Mexico's seat belt law applies to:
choices: Everyone in the vehicle, in every seat
questions.explanation: New Mexico requires every occupant, in every seating position, to be buckled up.

questions.prompt: New Mexico's seat belt law is enforced as a 'primary' law, which means:
choices: You can only be ticketed if stopped for something else
An officer can stop and ticket you for a belt violation alone
questions.explanation: Primary enforcement lets officers stop and cite a driver solely for a seat belt violation.

questions.prompt: In New Mexico, children must ride in an appropriate child safety seat until:
choices: Their 7th birthday, or under 60 pounds regardless of age
questions.explanation: New Mexico requires a child safety seat for children up to age 7 and for any child under 60 pounds.

questions.prompt: Refusing a lawful chemical test for alcohol in New Mexico results in:
questions.explanation: Under implied consent, refusing a chemical test leads to revocation of your driving privileges.

questions.prompt: Children ages 7 through 12 in New Mexico must use:
choices: A booster seat until the adult belt fits properly
questions.explanation: Older children must use a booster seat until the regular seat belt fits them correctly.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash, you must:
choices: Stop, give aid, and exchange information
Drive home and report it next week
questions.explanation: The law requires you to stop, help anyone injured, exchange information, and report the crash when required.

questions.prompt: Driving while intoxicated (DWI) in New Mexico can result in:
choices: Fines, license suspension or revocation, and possible jail
questions.explanation: DWI is a serious offense that can bring fines, loss of license, and jail time.

questions.prompt: Driving on a suspended or revoked license can lead to:
choices: Additional fines and penalties, including possible jail
questions.explanation: Driving while your license is suspended or revoked is itself a crime with added penalties.
